samples.user_query: Kids gymnastics programs near me
samples.assistant_text: Flips for Fun Gymnastics is your spot — they're at 955 Kiowa Ave and open Mon–Fri from 3pm to 8pm. Call (928) 566-8862 or check fffhavasu.com for class schedules and ages.

samples.user_query: Tell me about Bridge City Combat
samples.assistant_text: Bridge City Combat is a martial arts gym run by founder Christian Beyers at 2143 McCulloch Blvd N, Unit B. They're open until 9pm, though full weekly hours aren't confirmed yet. You'll need to book in person or reach out directly at (928) 716-3009. You can also find them on Instagram at instagram.com/bridgecitycombat for more details on classes and training.

route_meta.entity: Bridge City Combat (also: Bridge City Combat & Barry Sullins Jiu-Jitsu)

samples.user_query: Events at Sara Park
samples.assistant_text:
Sara Park's got solid action for BMX and mountain biking.

BMX-wise, there's weekly USA BMX-sanctioned races on Thursday nights (6–9pm, $10 entry), plus open practice Tuesday evenings (5–6:30pm, $5) and coached training Wednesday afternoons (5–6:15pm, $5). All skill levels welcome.

Mountain bikers can hit free practice sessions Sundays at 9am (more challenging terrain) or Mondays at 4:30pm through May 2026.

Everything's at 7260 Sara Park Lane. Kids as young as 4–5 can join most programs.

samples.user_query: BMX race times
samples.assistant_text:
The catalog doesn't have individual race times or results. What I can tell you is that Lake Havasu City BMX runs USA BMX-sanctioned races at SARA Park BMX Track (7260 Sara Park Lane).

Race night is Thursdays, 6–9pm (registration 6–7pm, racing starts 7pm). There are also weekly races on select dates in April 2026 at 6–7pm. Entry's $10.

For actual race times and results, call Lake Havasu City BMX at (928) 208-5388 — they'll have the details you need.

samples.user_query: Swimming lessons for beginners
samples.assistant_text: I've got Aqua Beginnings in the catalog — they run swim lessons at a private heated outdoor pool. Head to aquabeginnings.com to check their beginner program details and book.

samples.user_query: Dance classes for a 7-year-old
samples.assistant_text:
The rows I have show dance recitals and showcases, not ongoing classes. ACPA and Footlite School of Dance both appear active in Lake Havasu and offer programs across styles like ballet, jazz, and tap — which would suit a 7-year-old — but I don't have their class schedules or enrollment details here.

I'd suggest contacting them directly or checking their websites to ask about beginner classes for that age group.

samples.user_query: My son wants to ride mountain bikes. Any classes available?
samples.assistant_text: The Lake Havasu Mountain Bike Club runs free practices at Sara Park — there's one tonight (Wednesday) at 4:30–6pm, plus Sunday mornings at 9–10:30am and Monday afternoons at 4:30–6pm. Call (619) 823-5088 to confirm your son can join.

samples.user_query: Who teaches violin to adults?

samples.user_query: Underground techno tonight?
samples.assistant_text: I don't have underground techno or live music venues in the catalog — try https://www.golakehavasu.com/events or search "live music Lake Havasu tonight" to see what's posted.

samples.user_query: Sonics or Flips for fun for a shy 5-year-old?
samples.assistant_text: Flips for Fun's the better fit for a shy 5-year-old — they're open tonight at 3pm and gymnastics lets kids move at their own pace without the pressure of team dynamics. Sonics runs cheer and competitive gymnastics, which skews toward kids who're already confident in group settings.

samples.user_query: Bridge City Combat vs Footlite School of Dance for Saturday morning kids classes?
samples.assistant_text: I don't have Saturday morning class schedules for either Bridge City Combat or Footlite School of Dance in the catalog. Call Bridge City Combat at (928) 716-3009 or Footlite at (928) 854-4328 to ask about their weekend kids programs.

samples.user_query: Which martial arts gym has Saturday morning kids classes?

Call Arevalo Academy at (928) 855-0505 or Bridge City Combat at (928) 716-3009 to ask about Saturday kids classes.

samples.user_query: Best place for toddler tumbling
samples.assistant_text: Flips for Fun Gymnastics is your best bet for toddler tumbling — they're at 955 Kiowa Ave and open 3–8pm weekdays, so you can swing by after work. Call (928) 566-8862 or check fffhavasu.com to ask about their toddler classes and pricing.

samples.user_query: Compare Footlite and Ballet Havasu for preschool dance
samples.assistant_text: I've got Ballet Havasu in the catalog — they're at 2735 Maricopa Ave inside The Dance Center, (928) 412-8208, ballethavasu.org — but I don't have Footlite listed or preschool program details for either studio. Call Ballet Havasu directly or check their site to compare class schedules, ages, and pricing for the little ones.

samples.user_query: What's the best sushi in town?
samples.assistant_text: I don't have sushi restaurants in the catalog — try searching "sushi Lake Havasu" or check https://www.golakehavasu.com/ for current dining options.

samples.user_query: What should I do Saturday?
samples.assistant_text: Take the kids to Altitude Trampoline Park — it's open 9am–9pm Saturday, and a 90-minute open jump session is $19. They'll burn energy fast on the trampolines, and you can pick any time slot that works for you.

samples.user_query: Pick one thing to do with kids this weekend
samples.assistant_text: Go with Altitude Trampoline Park on Saturday — it's open 9am–9pm and a 90-minute open jump is $19, so the kids can burn energy without a big commitment. It's the quickest win for a weekend with younger kids.
